answer: The 7:45 start time makes mornings very stressful for our family. An 8:30 start would allow my kids to get enough sleep and still make our carpool work.
Research consistently supports later start times for high school students. I would like to see data on how this change will affect after-school activity schedules.
Changing the bell schedule will affect bus routing. Please coordinate with transportation before finalizing anything.
A later start time would make a huge difference for our household. Both parents work and getting two kids ready before 8 AM is genuinely difficult.
The revised proposal addresses most of my concerns from Round 1. I appreciate the district listening to parent input.
I support the 8:30 compromise. Please ensure the transition plan gives teachers adequate prep time before students arrive.
My daughter was told AP Biology was full but I know students from other neighborhoods got in. There needs to be a transparent lottery or priority system.
The district needs more bilingual staff who can communicate with Spanish-speaking families. Translation services at school events are inconsistent.
Several students in my class do not have reliable internet at home, which makes homework completion inequitable. A device lending program would help.
Students of color are receiving suspensions at a much higher rate than their peers for similar infractions. This needs to be publicly addressed by the superintendent.
Reducing non-instructional duties like lunch supervision and hall monitoring would give teachers meaningful time for collaboration and lesson planning.
My caseload is over 400 students. I cannot provide meaningful support to each student at that ratio. Hiring a second counselor for our school is urgently needed.
Access to more professional development specifically for paraprofessionals would help me better support students with IEPs.
A flexible work-from-home option one day a week for administrative staff would significantly improve morale without impacting student services.
